skill.name: arxiv-search
skill.description: Search arXiv for scholarly preprints and return relevant paper titles and abstracts.
skill.summary: Find arXiv preprints by topic and summarize returned titles and abstracts.
skill.author: langchain-ai

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography findings were false positives; the cited lines contain prose or argparse text, not cryptographic code. The skill has legitimate medium-risk behavior because it queries the external arXiv service and its documentation instructs agents to run Python and pip commands. No prompt injection attempt, credential access, hidden persistence, or malicious exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: External Network Query to arXiv
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The helper imports the arxiv package, creates an arXiv client, and requests search results from the external arXiv service. This matches the skill purpose and shows no malicious endpoint, but it still sends user search terms outside the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source creates an arXiv client and retrieves results, and the documentation states direct API access. The endpoint is legitimate for this research skill, so the risk is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Documented External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The documentation tells agents to run local Python commands and install the arxiv package with pip. These commands are expected for using the skill, but they create operational risk if an agent runs them without user approval or environment controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are explicit shell examples for running Python and pip. They are not hidden or obfuscated, but agents may execute them as part of normal skill use.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, but the cited lines are the skill description, arXiv explanatory text, documentation prose, and argparse description. No hashing, encryption, random number generation, or cryptographic algorithm use appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain natural language or argparse metadata only. There is no cryptographic API or algorithm call to evaluate.

low_findings.title: Hidden Home Directory Path References Are Benign
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged hidden home directory access because examples reference the .deepagents skill directory. The references are documentation paths for installed skills, not code that reads hidden files or accesses secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited paths appear only in examples and replacement instructions. No code opens, lists, modifies, or exfiltrates hidden directory contents.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request Through arXiv Client
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill performs a legitimate external search through the arxiv package, which may disclose user research queries to arXiv.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The implementation imports arxiv, creates a client, and iterates over client results. This is direct evidence of network-backed behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Command Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documentation includes shell commands for running the helper script and installing its dependency.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented command blocks are explicit and intended for normal use. They are transparent but still require command execution controls.

content.value_statement: Researchers need fast access to preprints that may not appear in journal indexes yet. This skill searches arXiv and returns relevant paper titles and abstracts for review.
actual_capabilities: Searches arXiv with a user-provided query string.
Limits returned papers with the max-papers argument.
Sorts results by relevance through the arxiv package.
Formats each result with a paper title and abstract summary.
Reports when the arxiv Python package is missing.
Uses arXiv without an API key.
limitations: Requires the arxiv Python package to be installed.
Sends search terms to the external arXiv service.
Returns arXiv metadata only, not full paper analysis.
The CLI entry point calls the search function but does not print the returned text.

use_cases.title: Survey Recent AI Papers
use_cases.description: Find relevant arXiv preprints for a new machine learning topic before starting a deeper review.

use_cases.title: Track Computational Biology Methods
use_cases.description: Search for bioinformatics, protein modeling, or genomics methods that may not be indexed elsewhere yet.

use_cases.title: Collect Candidate Citations
use_cases.description: Gather paper titles and abstracts for a thesis chapter, technical memo, or research planning document.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Topic Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for a small set of papers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search arXiv for papers about graph neural networks for molecules. Return five relevant titles and short abstract summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Research Areas
prompt_templates.prompt: Search arXiv for recent papers on diffusion models for protein design. Highlight common methods and application areas.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Reading List
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused literature collection for a project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find ten arXiv papers about single-cell RNA sequencing foundation models. Group the results by modeling approach.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Review Seed
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced research planning from retrieved papers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search arXiv for neural operators in climate modeling. Create a prioritized reading list with likely relevance to surrogate simulation.

output_examples.input: Search for deep learning drug discovery papers.
output_examples.output: A short list of matching arXiv papers, each with a title and abstract summary for quick screening.

output_examples.input: Find papers about genome assembly algorithms.
output_examples.output: Relevant preprints from arXiv with concise summaries that support a first-pass literature scan.

output_examples.input: Search for protein folding prediction methods.
output_examples.output: Paper titles and summaries sorted by relevance, suitable for choosing which abstracts to inspect next.

best_practices: Use specific technical terms and include the target domain in the query.
Start with a small max-papers value, then broaden after reviewing early results.
Verify important claims by reading the paper and checking publication status.
anti_patterns: Do not treat arXiv preprints as peer-reviewed final evidence.
Do not send private research ideas or sensitive project names as queries.
Do not use broad one-word queries when a precise phrase is available.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API key?
faq.answer: No. It uses the public arXiv service through the arxiv Python package.

faq.question: What fields does it return?
faq.answer: It formats each result with the paper title and abstract summary.

faq.question: Can it download full papers?
faq.answer: No. The provided helper searches metadata and summaries only.

faq.question: Which topics work best?
faq.answer: It works best for computer science, quantitative biology, statistics, physics, mathematics, and related arXiv categories.

faq.question: Does it work offline?
faq.answer: No. It needs network access to query arXiv.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning for external network queries and documented local command execution.
